planet_name,content: HD 189733 b,"HD 189733 b was discovered in October 2005 by Bouchy et al. using the ELODIE spectrograph at Haute-Provence Observatory (A&A 444, L15-L19). It is a hot Jupiter orbiting a bright K-dwarf star with a 2.2-day orbital period at ~19.3 parsecs. Knutson et al. 2007 (Nature 447, 183) used Spitzer 8-micron observations to construct the first day-night temperature map of any exoplanet. Wyttenbach et al. 2015 (A&A 577) reported a spectrally resolved sodium detection in the planet's atmosphere using HARPS. Poppenhaeger et al. 2013 reported a possible X-ray transit detection, the only such detection for any exoplanet to date."
content: Morton et al. 2016 validated Kepler-1167 b as part of a large-scale automated validation using false positive probability calculations for every Kepler Object of Interest. Out of 7056 KOIs, 1935 had probabilities less than 1% of being astrophysical false positives. Kepler-1167 b was among the 1284 newly validated planets. The validation used vespa, a publicly available Python package, and produced full stellar property posterior samplings for every host star modeled as single, binary, and triple systems.
